what: Target data format for all covariates
title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: standard representation used admove 3D array cov[x, y, t] first dimension indexes x (east–west), second dimension indexes y (north–south), third dimension indexes time. support plotting model setup, strongly recommended provide dimnames first two dimensions (ideally also time): dimnames(cov)[[1]]: x-coordinates (cell midpoints), dimnames(cov)[[2]]: y-coordinates (cell midpoints), dimnames(cov)[[3]]: time labels (preferably numeric times date strings).

previous_headings: Target data format for all covariates
title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: time vector describes start interval time slice used corresponds admove convention time since origin specified time reference information (admove_tref). example, time labels 0 1, slices valid first second time steps. date 0 corresponds specified tref()$origin time step 0 1 specified tref()$units. , now, can work dates, e.g. 2025-01-01 2025-04-01, decimal years, e.g. 2025.00 2025.25. case, admove uses first covariate field (including) 2025.25, second field 2025.25 onward. generally: slice assumed valid start time start time next slice.

what: A minimal example in target format
title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: simulate array four covariate fields target format.  array format target. practice, however, covariate data often arrive layouts. sections show use prep_cov() standardise common input formats.
code: ## Set seed for reproducibility set.seed(1)  ## Define grid midpoints x <- seq(-42.5, 42.5, by = 5) y <- seq(-72.5, 72.5, by = 5)  ## Define start times of time intervals tvals <- c(0, 1, 2, 3)  ## Simulate two smooth "basis" fields mat1 <- outer(x, y, function(xx, yy) {   exp(-((xx - 10)^2 + (yy + 5)^2) / (2 * 15^2)) }) + matrix(rnorm(length(x) * length(y), 0, 0.02),             nrow = length(x), ncol = length(y))  mat2 <- outer(x, y, function(xx, yy) {   exp(-((xx + 20)^2 + (yy - 20)^2) / (2 * 15^2)) }) + matrix(rnorm(length(x) * length(y), 0, 0.02),             nrow = length(x), ncol = length(y))  ## Build a time-varying covariate array as mixtures of mat1/mat2 arr <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(length(x), length(y), length(tvals))) for (k in seq_along(tvals)) {   p <- runif(2)   arr[, , k] <- p[1] * mat1 + p[2] * mat2 + 0.2 * sin(2 * pi * (k - 1) / length(tvals)) }  ## Attach dimnames (note: dimnames are character vectors by definition) dimnames(arr) <- list(   x = sprintf("%.2f", x),   y = sprintf("%.2f", y),   t = sprintf("%.2f", tvals) )  ## Plot two time slices par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 1)) image(x, y, arr[, , 1], main = paste("t =", dimnames(arr)[[3]][1]), xlab = "x", ylab = "y") image(x, y, arr[, , 2], main = paste("t =", dimnames(arr)[[3]][2]), xlab = "x", ylab = "y") par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

what: Common format A: a single field as a matrix
title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: covariates come single field effectively constant time, bathymetry (static) habitat classifications. use mat1 example:  can convert single matrix using prep_cov(): messages indicate, mat1 row/column names, prep_cov() must fall back row/column indices, rarely want. almost real workflows use x_centers y_centers arguments function: attach grid coordinates via rownames() colnames(): 2D matrix automatically promoted 3D array singleton time dimension (.e., t = 0):
code: image(x, y, mat1, main = "A single matrix (one time slice)", xlab = "x", ylab = "y") cov1 <- prep_cov(mat1) cov1 <- prep_cov(mat1, x_centers = x, y_centers = y) rownames(mat1) <- x colnames(mat1) <- y  cov1 <- prep_cov(mat1) dimnames(cov1)[[3]] #> [1] "0"

what: Common format B: multiple time slices as a 3D array
title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: Time-varying covariates (e.g., temperature) often provided 3D array whose third dimension time. already matches target representation, conversion straightforward: third-dimension labels describe start time interval (e.g., decimal years date strings). See section Converting dates decimal dates convenient date parsing.
code: cov2 <- prep_cov(arr) summary(cov2) #> <admove_cov> #>   cells:     540 #>   dims:      18 x 30 x 4 #>   cellsize:  5 x 5 #>   xrange:    [-45.00, 45.00] #>   yrange:    [-75.00, 75.00] #>   trange:    [0.00, 3.00] #>   cov range: [-0.23, 0.99] #>   NAs:       0 #>   units:     not specified x not specified dimnames(cov2)[[3]] #> [1] "0.00" "1.00" "2.00" "3.00"

previous_headings: Common format B: multiple time slices as a 3D array
what: Common format C: multiple time slices as a list of matrices
title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: Another common representation list element corresponds one time slice. create list array : Internally, list converted 3D array. list elements dimnames, add conversion coordinates time metadata carried correctly.
code: lst <- lapply(seq_len(dim(arr)[3]), function(k) arr[, , k, drop = FALSE]) cov3 <- prep_cov(lst) summary(cov3) #> <admove_cov> #>   cells:     540 #>   dims:      18 x 30 x 4 #>   cellsize:  5 x 5 #>   xrange:    [-45.00, 45.00] #>   yrange:    [-75.00, 75.00] #>   trange:    [0.00, 3.00] #>   cov range: [-0.23, 0.99] #>   NAs:       0 #>   units:     not specified x not specified

title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: Many covariate products distributed rasters. raster package available, prep_cov() supports RasterLayer objects directly (Hijmans 2025). section shows small example using simulated data .
code: install.packages("raster") library(raster)  dx <- diff(x)[1] dy <- diff(y)[1]  ## Define raster extent using cell edges (centers +/- half a cell) r <- raster::raster(   xmn = min(x) - dx/2, xmx = max(x) + dx/2,   ymn = min(y) - dy/2, ymx = max(y) + dy/2,   res = c(dx, dy),   crs = NA )  stopifnot(ncol(r) == length(x), nrow(r) == length(y))

title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: frequent source mistakes raster objects R matrices use different conventions y-axis oriented values stored vector. ensure raster matches matrix plot see image(), flip matrix y-direction assigning values:  Now raster can converted directly: prefer depend raster inside workflow, can always convert raster-like objects outside admove pass matrix/array prep_cov().
code: mat_topdown <- arr[, , 1][, ncol(arr[, , 1]):1, drop = FALSE] raster::values(r) <- as.vector(mat_topdown)  par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 1)) image(x, y, arr[, , 1], main = "Standard matrix (image)", xlab = "x", ylab = "y") plot(r, main = "RasterLayer (raster)") par(mfrow = c(1, 1)) cov_r <- prep_cov(r) summary(cov_r) #> <admove_cov> #>   cells:     540 #>   dims:      18 x 30 x 1 #>   cellsize:  5 x 5 #>   xrange:    [-45.00, 45.00] #>   yrange:    [-75.00, 75.00] #>   trange:    [0.00, 0.00] #>   cov range: [-0.06, 0.99] #>   NAs:       0 #>   units:     not specified x not specified

title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: terra package increasingly common raster data (GeoTIFF, NetCDF, etc.) (Hijmans 2026). terra available, admove can convert SpatRaster objects directly.
code: install.packages("terra") library(terra)  dx <- diff(x)[1] dy <- diff(y)[1]  t <- terra::rast(   ncols = length(x),   nrows = length(y),   xmin  = min(x) - dx/2, xmax = max(x) + dx/2,   ymin  = min(y) - dy/2, ymax = max(y) + dy/2,   crs   = "" )  ## Use the same orientation adjustment as in the raster example mat_topdown <- arr[, , 1][, ncol(arr[, , 1]):1, drop = FALSE] terra::values(t) <- as.vector(mat_topdown)  plot(t, main = "terra SpatRaster") cov_t <- prep_cov(t) summary(cov_t) #> <admove_cov> #>   cells:     540 #>   dims:      18 x 30 x 1 #>   cellsize:  5 x 5 #>   xrange:    [-45.00, 45.00] #>   yrange:    [-75.00, 75.00] #>   trange:    [0.00, 0.00] #>   cov range: [-0.06, 0.99] #>   NAs:       0 #>   units:     not specified x not specified

title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: Time-varying fields can represented multi-layer SpatRaster objects (one layer per time slice). can also converted prep_cov().
code: t_stack <- c(t, t)  ## Layer names should ideally encode time information names(t_stack) <- c("2025.00", "2025.50")  cov_t_stack <- prep_cov(t_stack, date_decimal = TRUE) summary(cov_t_stack) #>         Length Class      Mode    #> 2025.00 540    admove_cov numeric #> 2025.50 540    admove_cov numeric

title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: Many oceanographic atmospheric products distributed NetCDF files. often store variables arrays dimensions lon × lat × time (x × y × time). prep_cov() read NetCDF files directly, can read variable ncdf4 pass matrix/array prep_cov() (Pierce 2025). create small dummy NetCDF file demonstrate workflow. Now read file, extract coordinates time metadata, attach dimnames admove convention [x, y, t]:
code: install.packages("ncdf4") library(ncdf4)  tmpfile <- tempfile(fileext = ".nc")  ## Define time stamps as Dates (start of each interval) t_dates <- as.Date(c("2025-01-01", "2025-04-01", "2025-07-01", "2025-10-01"))  ## NetCDF dimensions dim_x <- ncdim_def("x", "km", vals = x) dim_y <- ncdim_def("y", "km", vals = y)  ## NetCDF time is usually numeric with a units string time_units <- "days since 1970-01-01" time_vals  <- as.numeric(t_dates - as.Date("1970-01-01")) dim_t <- ncdim_def("time", time_units, vals = time_vals)  var_cov <- ncvar_def(   name = "cov",   units = "1",   dim = list(dim_x, dim_y, dim_t),   missval = NA_real_,   longname = "dummy covariate" )  nc <- nc_create(tmpfile, vars = list(var_cov)) ncvar_put(nc, var_cov, arr) nc_close(nc) nc <- nc_open(tmpfile)  x_read    <- ncvar_get(nc, "x") y_read    <- ncvar_get(nc, "y") time_read <- ncvar_get(nc, "time")  ## numeric days since 1970-01-01 cov_read  <- ncvar_get(nc, "cov")  nc_close(nc)  t_read <- as.Date(time_read, origin = "1970-01-01")  dimnames(cov_read) <- list(   x = sprintf("%.2f", x_read),   y = sprintf("%.2f", y_read),   t = format(t_read) )  cov_nc <- prep_cov(cov_read, date_format = "%Y-%m-%d") summary(cov_nc) #> <admove_cov> #>   cells:     540 #>   dims:      18 x 30 x 4 #>   cellsize:  5 x 5 #>   xrange:    [-45.00, 45.00] #>   yrange:    [-75.00, 75.00] #>   trange:    [0.43, 39.43] #>              [2025-01-01 00:11:52 #>                  2025-10-01 18:11:52] #>   cov range: [-0.23, 0.99] #>   NAs:       0 #>   units:     not specified x week

title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: Real products often use different dimension names orders, e.g. lon × lat × time, time × lat × lon, include additional dimensions depth. cases typically need : identify dimensions correspond x/lon y/lat, select/aggregate depth (present), permute array [x, y, t] using aperm(). example, array [time, y, x], : attach dimnames(cov_xy_t) <- list(x=..., y=..., t=...) calling prep_cov() use arguments x_centers, y_centers times.
code: cov_xy_t <- aperm(cov_read, c(3, 2, 1))  ## (time, y, x) -> (x, y, time)

what: Converting date strings and decimal years to numeric time
title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: third dimension uses date strings, prep_cov() can parse via date_format date_origin convert numeric time scale (e.g., weeks since origin) used internally. example, monthly fields labelled "YYYY-MM-DD":
code: months <- c("2020-01-01", "2020-04-01", "2020-07-01", "2020-10-01")  arr_month <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(length(x), length(y), length(months))) for (k in seq_along(months)) {   arr_month[, , k] <- mat1 + 0.1 * k }  dimnames(arr_month) <- list(   x = sprintf("%.2f", x),   y = sprintf("%.2f", y),   t = months )  cov_month <- prep_cov(arr_month, date_format = "%Y-%m-%d") dimnames(cov_month)[[3]] #> [1] "0.428571428571429" "13.4285714285714"  "26.4285714285714"  #> [4] "39.5714285714286"

title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: time reference information important stores origin time 0 corresponds units defines 1 corresponds one day, week, month, year, etc. reason, prep_cov() includes tref argument allows specifying important variables. example, can prepare covariate fields corresponding tref information Now, numeric values dimnames(cov2)[[3]] misinterpreted longer time reference information clear: information also provided summary: course, information can also added preparation covariate fields: dates decimal years provided rather numeric value, prep_cov() try infer time reference information. example, cov_nc cov_t_stack information inferred data: Nevertheless, recommend specify tref covariate fields explicitely. tref information inferred correctly, shift_tref() scale_tref() functions allow correct information: Now, covariate fields corresponds months starting January 1st 2024:
code: cov2 <- prep_cov(arr,                  tref = list(origin = "2020-01-01",                             units = "quarter")) tref(cov2) #> $origin #> [1] "2020-01-01 CET" #>  #> $units #> [1] "quarter" #>  #> $period #> [1] 4 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "admove_tref" summary(cov2) #> <admove_cov> #>   cells:     540 #>   dims:      18 x 30 x 4 #>   cellsize:  5 x 5 #>   xrange:    [-45.00, 45.00] #>   yrange:    [-75.00, 75.00] #>   trange:    [0.00, 3.00] #>              [2020-01-01 00:00:00 #>                  2020-09-30 19:00:00] #>   cov range: [-0.23, 0.99] #>   NAs:       0 #>   units:     not specified x quarter cov2 <- prep_cov(arr)  cov2 <- add_tref(cov2, list(origin = "2020-01-01",                               units = "quarter"))  tref(cov2) #> $origin #> [1] "2020-01-01 CET" #>  #> $units #> [1] "quarter" #>  #> $period #> [1] 4 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "admove_tref" tref(cov_nc) tref(cov_t_stack) cov2 <- shift_tref(cov2, origin = "2024-01-01")  cov2 <- scale_tref(cov2, units = "month") tref(cov2) #> $origin #> [1] "2024-01-01" #>  #> $units #> [1] "month" #>  #> $period #> [1] 12 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "admove_tref"

title: Preparing covariates for admove
text: Similar time reference information, spatial information gridded covariate fields also refers certain coordinate system (CRS) units. Therefore, covariate fields also contain sref object. code: format_a <- tracks colnames(format_a) <- c("time", "lon", "lat", "tag_id") head(format_a) #>   time  lon  lat  tag_id #> 1    0 12.0 55.0 tag_001 #> 2    1 12.1 55.0 tag_001 #> 3    2 12.2 55.1 tag_001 #> 4    3 12.2 55.2 tag_001 #> 5    4 12.3 55.2 tag_001 #> 6    5 12.4 55.3 tag_001 dtags <- prep_dtags(   x = format_a,   names = c(t = "time",             x = "lon",             y = "lat",             id = "tag_id") )  head(dtags) #>   t    x    y      id tag_type use #> 1 0 12.0 55.0 tag_001        d   1 #> 2 1 12.1 55.0 tag_001        d   1 #> 3 2 12.2 55.1 tag_001        d   1 #> 4 3 12.2 55.2 tag_001        d   1 #> 5 4 12.3 55.2 tag_001        d   1 #> 6 5 12.4 55.3 tag_001        d   1

Prepare mark-recapture ctags <- prep_tags(skjepo$ctags,                    tag_type = "c",                    names = c(t0 = "date_time", t1 = "date_caught",                              x0 = "rel_lon", x1 = "recap_lon",                              y0 = "rel_lat", y1 = "recap_lat"),                    date_origin = "1899-12-30",                    sref = list(crs = 4326),                    tref = list(origin = as.POSIXct("2020-01-01", "UTC"),                                units = "month"))  ## Load dtags dtags <- prep_tags(skjepo$dtags,                    tag_type = "d",                    names = c(t = "time", x = "mptlon", y = "mptlat"),                    date_origin = "1899-12-30")  ## Adjust the space reference information dtags <- add_sref(dtags, list(crs = 4326))  ## Adjust the time reference information dtags <- add_tref(dtags, list(origin = as.POSIXct("2020-01-01", "UTC"),                               units = "month"), shift_origin = TRUE) ## Summarise the tagging information summary(c(ctags, dtags)) #> <admove_tags> #>   tags total:     220 #>   --------------------------------- #>   data-storage tags #>   n:              20 #>   average over ids: #>   n obs:          108.15 #>   duration:       10.74 #>   time step:      0.10 #>   x step:         -0.04 #>   y step:         -0.02 #>   --------------------------------- #>   mark-recapture tags #>   n:              200 #>   average over ids: #>   n obs:          2.00 #>   duration:       10.20 #>   time step:      10.20 #>   x step:         -2.59 #>   y step:         -1.27 #>   --------------------------------- #>   crs:            WGS 84 [EPSG:4326] #>   crs units:      degree #>   stored units:   degree #>   crs scale:      1 degree = 1 degree #>   origin:         2020-01-01 UTC #>   units:          month #>   period:         12 ## Define the target time reference information tref_target <- create_tref(origin = as.POSIXct("2020-01-01", "UTC"),                            units = "month")  ## Define the target space reference information using Azimuthal Equidistant ## centered at (−110, 0) sref_target <- create_sref(crs = "+proj=aeqd +lat_0=0 +lon_0=-110 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs",                              units = "km")  ## Adjust the reference info for the tags ctags <- add_sref(ctags, sref_target, transform_crs = TRUE) ctags <- add_tref(ctags, tref_target, shift_origin = TRUE)  dtags <- add_sref(dtags, sref_target, transform_crs = TRUE) dtags <- add_tref(dtags, tref_target, shift_origin = TRUE) summary(c(ctags, dtags)) #> <admove_tags> #>   tags total:     220 #>   --------------------------------- #>   data-storage tags #>   n:              20 #>   average over ids: #>   n obs:          108.15 #>   duration:       10.74 #>   time step:      0.10 #>   x step:         -4.80 #>   y step:         -1.76 #>   --------------------------------- #>   mark-recapture tags #>   n:              200 #>   average over ids: #>   n obs:          2.00 #>   duration:       10.20 #>   time step:      10.20 #>   x step:         -285.87 #>   y step:         -140.05 #>   --------------------------------- #>   crs:            Azimuthal Equidistant [custom] #>   datum:          World Geodetic System 1984 #>   crs units:      metre #>   stored units:   km #>   crs scale:      1 metre = 0.001 km #>   origin:         2020-01-01 UTC #>   units:          month #>   period:         12 grid <- create_grid(skjepo$grid, cellsize = 750) plot(grid, plot_land = TRUE) ## covariates cov <- prep_cov(skjepo$cov) plot_cov(cov[,,1:4], plot_land = TRUE) tref(cov) #> $origin #> [1] "2020-01-01 UTC" #>  #> $units #> [1] "quarter" #>  #> $period #> [1] 4 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "admove_tref" ## combine and check data dat <- setup_data(grid = grid,                   cov = cov,                   tags = c(ctags, dtags),                   tref = tref_target,                   shift_tref = TRUE) ## Default configurations conf <- default_conf(dat) ## Default parameters par <- default_par(dat, conf) par$logKappa <- skjepo$sim$par_sim$logKappa

title: Estimating animal movement with advection–diffusion models
text: admove new R package tagging-based movement modelling, developed estimate fine-scale animal movement patterns range tagging data, including mark-recapture, mark-resight, data-logging (archival) tags. underlying movement model based advection-diffusion equation can incorporate habitat preference functions, allowing reconstruction individual movement paths well inference population-level movement dynamics relatively small set interpretable parameters. Movement rates can modelled function environmental conditions geographic location. current version package supports estimation habitat preference functions movement patterns. Future development extend framework include spatiotemporally varying recapture probability, natural fishing mortality, well relative biomass indices spatial distributions. get started admove, install load package, simulate tagging dataset, fit movement model:
code: ## install required packages install.packages("remotes")  ## install admove from local source remotes::install_github("tokami/admove")  ## Load admove library(admove)  ## Simulate a small tagging data set sim <- sim_data()  ## Fit admove to the simulated data fit <- admove(sim)  ## Plot simulated data and model predictions plot_compare(list(sim = sim, fit = fit))

text: lightweight container stores time reference information used throughout admove. bundles: origin: POSIXct date-time used temporal origin units: units numeric time axis stored objects period: optional seasonal cycle length units stored times

title: Time reference for admove objects — create_tref
code: create_tref(origin = NA, units = NA_character_, period = NULL)

title: Time reference for admove objects — create_tref
text: origin date-time corresponding temporal origin (e.g. release date). converted POSIXct. Use NA leave undefined. units Character string describing units stored numeric time axis, e.g. "year", "quarter", "month", "week", "day". Use list_units_time() supported options. period Optional numeric seasonal period (cycle length) units stored time axis. NULL, default inferred common annual discretizations units known.

title: Time reference for admove objects — create_tref
text: object class admove_tref.

title: Time reference for admove objects — create_tref
text: intention objects store times simple numeric axis (e.g. months since origin), origin units define values interpreted displayed. period provided (can inferred), can used create seasonally repeating covariate fields spline bases wrapping time modulo arithmetic. example, times stored months, natural annual seasonal cycle period = 12. custom discretizations (e.g. 10 time steps per year), set period = 10 units = "custom". units missing period supplied, units can inferred common annual discretizations (period = 1, 2, 4, 12, 52) otherwise defaults "custom".

title: Time reference for admove objects — create_tref
code: ## Monthly time axis with annual seasonality (12 months) tr <- create_tref(origin = as.Date("2025-01-01"), units = "month") tr #> $origin #> [1] "2025-01-01 UTC" #>  #> $units #> [1] "month" #>  #> $period #> [1] 12 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "admove_tref"  ## Infer units from period tr2 <- create_tref(origin = as.Date("2025-01-01"), period = 12) tr2 #> $origin #> [1] "2025-01-01 UTC" #>  #> $units #> [1] "month" #>  #> $period #> [1] 12 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "admove_tref"  ## Custom discretization: 10 time steps per year tr10 <- create_tref(origin = as.Date("2025-01-01"), period = 10) tr10 #> $origin #> [1] "2025-01-01 UTC" #>  #> $units #> [1] "custom" #>  #> $period #> [1] 10 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "admove_tref"

what: Convert dates to numeric time since an origin — date_2_time

code: date_2_time(dates, tref = NULL)

title: Convert dates to numeric time since an origin — date_2_time
text: dates vector class Date, POSIXct, POSIXlt. tref Optional time reference. One : NULL: infer origin units dates. single Date POSIXct/POSIXlt: use origin infer units dates. list element origin optional element units, e.g. list(origin = .Date("2020-01-01"), units = "day").

title: Convert dates to numeric time since an origin — date_2_time
text: numeric vector length dates. vector attribute "tref", list elements: origin reference origin used. units time units used numeric scale. floor_unit unit used floor inferred origin, relevant. inferred Logical; whether reference inferred.

title: Convert dates to numeric time since an origin — date_2_time
text: tref supplied, function uses reference. tref = NULL, function infers sensible reference input: origin based earliest non-missing date (optionally floored convenient boundary), time units guessed overall time range. returned value numeric vector attribute "tref" containing reference used. makes easy reuse reference later, e.g. calling date_2_time(new_dates, tref = attr(x, "tref")). units "month" "year", conversion calendar-aware uses lubridate::time_length() interval rather fixed-day approximations.

title: Convert dates to numeric time since an origin — date_2_time
code: d <- as.Date(c("2020-01-15", "2020-02-01", "2020-03-10"))  x <- date_2_time(d) x #> [1]  0 17 55 #> attr(,"tref") #> attr(,"tref")$origin #> [1] "2020-01-15" #>  #> attr(,"tref")$units #> [1] "day" #>  #> attr(,"tref")$floor_unit #> [1] "day" #>  #> attr(,"tref")$inferred #> [1] TRUE #>  attr(x, "tref") #> $origin #> [1] "2020-01-15" #>  #> $units #> [1] "day" #>  #> $floor_unit #> [1] "day" #>  #> $inferred #> [1] TRUE #>   ## Reuse the same reference date_2_time(d, tref = attr(x, "tref")) #> [1]  0 17 55 #> attr(,"tref") #> attr(,"tref")$origin #> [1] "2020-01-15" #>  #> attr(,"tref")$units #> [1] "day" #>  #> attr(,"tref")$floor_unit #> [1] "day" #>  #> attr(,"tref")$inferred #> [1] TRUE #>   ## Explicit origin and units date_2_time(d, tref = list(origin = as.Date("2020-01-01"), units = "day")) #> [1] 14 31 69 #> attr(,"tref") #> attr(,"tref")$origin #> [1] "2020-01-01" #>  #> attr(,"tref")$units #> [1] "day" #>  #> attr(,"tref")$floor_unit #> [1] "day" #>  #> attr(,"tref")$inferred #> [1] FALSE #>   z <- as.POSIXct(c("2020-01-01 00:00:00",                   "2020-01-01 12:00:00",                   "2020-01-02 06:00:00"),                 tz = "UTC") date_2_time(z, tref = list(origin = z[1], units = "hour")) #> [1]  0 12 30 #> attr(,"tref") #> attr(,"tref")$origin #> [1] "2020-01-01 UTC" #>  #> attr(,"tref")$units #> [1] "hour" #>  #> attr(,"tref")$floor_unit #> [1] "hour" #>  #> attr(,"tref")$inferred #> [1] FALSE #>

text: function generates named list initial parameter values based supplied data model configuration. values can used starting values model fitting intended provide reasonable defaults selected model setup. taxis scaling parameter logKappa fixed estimation (see default_map()) therefore initial value final value. kappa units \([\text{distance}^2 / \text{time}]\), value 1 appropriate coordinates already unit scale. projected coordinates UTM (metres), kappa = 1 makes taxis contribution negligible renders taxis spline coefficients unidentifiable. default therefore set kappa = cellsize^2 / median_dt, ensures unit covariate gradient one grid-cell width produces movement one cell width per median time step. title: Shift the origin of a time reference while preserving represented dates — shift_tref
text: function used object already valid tref want adopt different origin without changing underlying time points numeric values represent. words, changes coordinate system time axis, actual dates. new origin can supplied directly via origin, indirectly via tref tref-like object another object tref(tref) can extracted. Let \(t\) denote stored numeric time values, \(o_0\) old origin, \(o_1\) new origin. preserve represented dates, new time values \(t'\) must satisfy: $$o_0 + t = o_1 + t'$$ therefore $$t' = t - (o_1 - o_0).$$ offset \(o_1 - o_0\) computed current tref units. "second", "minute", "hour", "day", "week", fixed-duration differences used. title: admove 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: admove 0.1.0
text: Fits advection–diffusion movement models tagging data using RTMB-based automatic differentiation; compiled C++ code required. Supports three tag types: archival/data-logging tags (prep_dtags()), mark–recapture tags (prep_ctags()), mark–resight tags (prep_stags()). Multiple tag types can fitted jointly. Two estimation engines selectable via conf$engine: Kalman filter (engine 1): continuous space, discrete time. Continuous-time Markov chain (engine 2): discrete space, continuous time, using matrix exponentiation transition-rate matrix. Habitat preference functions taxis (directed movement), diffusion (random movement intensity), advection represented flexible polynomial splines fitted covariate fields. setup_data() assembles covariate grids, tag observations, prediction grids single data list model fitting. default_conf(), default_par(), default_map() provide sensible starting configurations; can modified calling admove(). Post-processing helpers add_sdreport(), add_report(), add_predictions() attach uncertainty estimates, RTMB-reported quantities, grid-level predictions fitted object. Simulation framework (sim_data(), sim_tags(), sim_cov()) generating synthetic datasets specified movement parameters. Spatial temporal reference system attached major objects via sref / tref attributes corresponding S3 generics. Visualisation: plot_fit(), plot_compare(), plot_pref_func(), plot_pref_grid(), plot_taxis(), plot_diffusion(), plot_tags(), plot_taxis(), plot_cov(), plot_land(). Introductory vignette full function documentation.
